name: PaperBench
aliases: PaperBench: Evaluating AI's Ability to Replicate AI Research
current_status: Active public benchmark with an official paper, OpenAI release page, and open repository.
positioning: Benchmark for end-to-end AI research replication from paper to code.
boundary_notes: Included because it evaluates long-horizon AI research engineering rather than narrow coding or question answering. Each task requires understanding a paper, building a codebase, executing experiments, and being graded against a rubric.
tool_access: API access for agents and judge
execution_environment: Three-stage containerized evaluation: agent rollout in an Ubuntu container, reproduction in a fresh GPU container, and grading in a third container.
human_roles: Benchmark designers select the papers and co-develop rubrics with the original paper authors. Users configure the benchmark, provide API keys and compute, run agents, and interpret the scores.
autonomy_rationale: PaperBench targets agents that must independently read papers, build codebases, run experiments, and iterate over failures, but still operate inside benchmark-provided infrastructure and evaluation scaffolding.
iteration_loop: Evaluated agents are expected to iterate over implementation and experiments; the benchmark itself runs rollout, reproduction, and grading as separate stages.
stopping_and_guardrails: Container isolation, explicit rubrics, separate grading infrastructure, required API-key configuration, and judge-based scoring constrain execution and evaluation.
known_failure_modes: incomplete reproduction of a paper's contributions
benchmarks_used: PaperBench itself, plus a separate benchmark for evaluating the LLM judge.
metrics: judge quality on a separate judge benchmark
baselines: BasicAgent and IterativeAgent variants on frontier models
top ML PhD human baseline on a subset
validation_strength: Benchmarked with hierarchical rubrics co-developed with the original paper authors and compared against a human baseline.
evaluation_limitations: Scope is limited to 20 ICML 2024 Spotlight and Oral papers, evaluation depends partly on an LLM judge, and results may not generalize to all research domains or non-ICML workflows [uncertain].
source_quality: Primary sources: official OpenAI benchmark page, official arXiv paper, and official repository.
predecessors: [uncertain] No explicit predecessor benchmark is named in the consulted primary sources.
successors: [uncertain] No formal successor benchmark was identified in the consulted primary sources.
evaluated_by: [uncertain] No separate external benchmark evaluating PaperBench itself was identified in the consulted sources.
milestones: 2025: Public repository released with benchmark data, code, setup instructions, and leaderboard section.
version_history: public repository documentation includes setup, data hydration, leaderboard, and judge-evaluation instructions
adoption_signals: Public official repository with a leaderboard section
supersession_notes: No supersession was noted in the consulted primary sources.
real_world_applications: evaluating autonomous agents for research engineering
studying long-horizon coding and experiment workflows
deployment_constraints: Requires containerized execution, GPU access for reproduction, hydrated Git-LFS data, and API keys for the agents and judge. Some papers also require access to the OpenAI API and Hugging Face.
cost_and_runtime: Repository materials mention 24-hour and 36-hour evaluation limits for some leaderboard runs. Actual cost and runtime depend on model APIs, GPU access, and benchmark configuration [uncertain].
scientific_integrity_risks: over-interpreting benchmark scores as general research ability
security_and_safety_risks: API key exposure if environments are misconfigured
GPU or container misuse and unsafe external downloads during replication [uncertain]
mitigations: co-developed rubrics with original paper authors
explicit environment and API-key setup instructions
policy_relevance: Relevant to frontier-model preparedness, measurement of long-horizon autonomous AI research capabilities, and the design of evaluations for increasingly capable coding and research agents.
